_meta.purpose: Pre-computed cultural anchors per year, used by record-type generators in src/voice/generator.py to ground 'last time it was this hot in YEAR' framings without asking Gemini to invent one.
_meta.selection_criteria: Universally recognizable, time-marking (triggers feeling-of-elapsed-time, not fact lookup), globally legible (not US-only when avoidable), politically neutral, not mass-tragedy-as-scaffolding.
_meta.format: year (string) -> list of short anchor strings, each ready to slot into 'back when X' / 'the year Y' / 'still when Z' framings.
_meta.year_coverage: 1995-2025. Open-Meteo archive starts ~1995; pre-1995 records are exceedingly rare in this pipeline.
_meta.tuning: 8 anchors per year is the design target — enough variety so the same year never gets the same framing twice in a row, few enough that curation stays high-quality.

1995: Toy Story released as the first all-CGI feature
the OJ Simpson trial dominated TV
1996: the Spice Girls hit number one
Mac and PC were still actually fighting
the Tickle Me Elmo craze peaked
Independence Day was the summer movie
1997: Princess Diana's death stopped the world
Hong Kong was returned to China
Titanic was about to break every box-office record
Deep Blue beat Kasparov at chess
the Spice Girls were the biggest band on earth
1998: the iMac was the future of computing
Saving Private Ryan changed war movies
the euro currency wasn't in circulation yet
1999: Napster launched and broke the music industry
Star Wars Episode I came out
the dot-com boom was at its peak
Prince's '1999' was finally on the radio for real
the Euro had just been introduced as accounting currency
Nokia phones were the cool ones
2000: Y2K turned out to be fine
Survivor premiered and reality TV took off
Erin Brockovich was the movie everyone discussed
George W. Bush vs. Al Gore went to the courts
2001: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone hit theaters
Apple opened its first retail store
the September 11 attacks reshaped the world
Lord of the Rings: Fellowship of the Ring opened
2002: the Salt Lake City Olympics happened
Lord of the Rings: Two Towers came out
Friendster was the first big social network
2003: Finding Nemo was the summer hit
2004: the Boston Red Sox won the World Series after 86 years
the Indian Ocean tsunami hit on Boxing Day
2005: Pride and Prejudice with Keira Knightley came out
Crash won Best Picture in an upset
2006: Pluto was demoted from planet status
Facebook opened to anyone with an email
The Office (US version) was hitting its stride
Italy won the World Cup in Berlin
2007: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows ended the series
the Great Recession warning signs appeared
Live Free or Die Hard came out
2008: The Dark Knight broke every record
Iron Man kicked off the MCU
Heath Ledger died before The Dark Knight came out
2009: swine flu was the global pandemic scare
Susan Boyle made everyone cry on Britain's Got Talent
Twitter hit mainstream during the Iran protests
2010: Deepwater Horizon spilled into the Gulf for months
Inception had everyone arguing about the ending
the Chilean miner rescue captivated the world
the Eyjafjallajökull volcano grounded European flights
2011: the Arab Spring swept the Middle East
Fukushima nuclear disaster followed the Tohoku earthquake
Game of Thrones premiered on HBO
Adele's 21 became the bestselling album of the decade
2012: the Higgs boson was confirmed at CERN
Avengers assembled for the first time
2013: Edward Snowden leaked the NSA files
Frozen took over every parent's house
2014: the Ebola outbreak hit West Africa
the ALS Ice Bucket Challenge took over Facebook
the World Cup in Brazil opened
2015: Star Wars: The Force Awakens revived the franchise
the US legalized same-sex marriage nationwide
2016: Pokémon GO had everyone walking outside
the Cubs won the World Series after 108 years
Prince and David Bowie both died
Hamilton was the hottest ticket on Broadway
2017: the Total Solar Eclipse crossed America
2018: the Thai cave rescue captivated the world
the Florence and the Machine concert tour was huge
BTS broke into the US mainstream
A Star Is Born remade brought back power ballads
2019: Joker was the cultural lightning rod
the first photo of a black hole was published
Greta Thunberg crossed the Atlantic by sailboat
2020: Tiger King was a pandemic phenomenon
Among Us was the lockdown game
stay-at-home was a way of life
2021: the Suez Canal got blocked by the Ever Given
the Tokyo Olympics finally happened, in 2021
Britney Spears was finally freed from her conservatorship
the James Webb Space Telescope launched
2022: Queen Elizabeth II died after 70 years on the throne
Wordle was bought by the New York Times
the World Cup happened in Qatar
ChatGPT was released to the public
Twitter was acquired by Elon Musk
2023: ChatGPT became part of everyday life
the Hollywood writers' strike shut down production
Barbie and Oppenheimer opened the same weekend
Taylor Swift's Eras Tour grossed over a billion
the OpenAI board fired Sam Altman, then unfired him
2024: the Paris Olympics opened on the Seine
Trump won the US election a second time
the SpaceX Starship caught its booster mid-air
the total solar eclipse crossed North America
Taylor Swift kept dating Travis Kelce
2025: DeepSeek made the world rethink AI costs
the Bluesky exodus from X accelerated
the LA wildfires devastated Pacific Palisades
Sora 2 made AI video the default
Studio Ghibli AI art took over the internet
